title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
summary: MCP and ChatGPT apps use double nested iframes to sandbox third-party code while avoiding CSP violations and storage isolation problems. The outer iframe loads a lightweight script from a controlled subdomain, which loads the actual app HTML via srcdoc into the inner frame. This pattern parallels Facebook's app marketplace architecture and requires declaring all external domains in app metadata.
writeup: Frédéric Barthelet of Alpic explains why MCP and ChatGPT apps render third-party code inside nested iframes. An outer iframe loads a lightweight script from a controlled subdomain, which then injects the actual app HTML into an inner iframe via srcdoc, isolating untrusted code while preserving security guarantees. The talk details how this structure prevents CSP violations, localStorage leaks, and sandbox escapes, and notes that each external domain must be declared in app metadata to pass submission review. The takeaway is a concrete account of the same-origin policy and sandbox attribute mechanics behind app embedding.
